seoTitle: Use Mermaid Diagrams in Confluence: 2026 Code Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to use Mermaid diagrams in Confluence in 2026, from writing flowchart syntax in a code block to switching themes and publishing live graphs.
intro: Static image uploads break the moment your architecture changes, forcing constant re-exports from separate design tools. This guide shows how to write live, text-based Mermaid diagrams directly inside a Confluence code block in 2026.

steps.heading: Open a code block
steps.body: In edit mode, type `/code` to deploy a text frame and select Mermaid from the language dropdown.

steps.heading: Set the chart direction
steps.body: Type `graph TD` to set a top-down direction, then write out your system nodes and text labels.

steps.heading: Connect your nodes
steps.body: Use arrow connectors to link individual step IDs together into a clean relational flow.

steps.heading: Preview the render
steps.body: Click the macro's display tab to toggle between raw code view and the live rendered preview.

steps.heading: Adjust theme and publish
steps.body: Tweak alignment in the bottom toolbar, switch the visual theme if needed, then hit Publish.

faq.question: Do I need a marketplace plugin to use Mermaid diagrams in Confluence?
faq.answer: Native code blocks support Mermaid syntax directly, though plugins like Mermaid Chart can add extra editing features.

faq.question: Will my diagram update automatically if I change the code?
faq.answer: Yes, editing the Mermaid text and republishing updates the rendered graph without needing to redraw anything manually.

faq.question: Can I change the color theme of my diagram?
faq.answer: Yes, macro switches let you instantly transform a chart into dark mode, forest tones, or custom brand colors.

faq.question: Can Mermaid handle complex diagrams like sequence flows or database structures?
faq.answer: Yes, Mermaid syntax supports sequence timelines, database structures, and microservice relationship maps.
